qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Restricted API tokens inherit full account permissions
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets API token permission restoration in authenticated middleware and admin/API routes, not the advisory's public share-info response leaking a persisted tokenized download URL for password-protected shares.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Write-enabled public shares can escape their shared subtree
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets traversal through public share resource mutation handlers after path joining and sanitization mistakes, not the advisory's GET /public/api/share/info disclosure of an already-persisted tokenized DownloadURL.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: WebDAV bypasses descendant access-control rules
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets missing descendant authorization in the /dav WebDAV handler, not the advisory's public share-info endpoint returning a bearer download URL for password-protected shares.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No accepted qualifying finding identifies the advisory's root cause: GET /public/api/share/info returning a persisted tokenized downloadURL for password-protected shares, which then enables unauthenticated download.
evidence: The advisory describes a disclosure in the public share-info endpoint that reflects a persisted DownloadURL containing token= for password-protected shares.
api-token-permission-bypass concerns JWT/API token permission collapse in authenticated middleware and does not involve public share handlers, password-protected shares, or DownloadURL reflection.

public-share-path-escape concerns traversal in PATCH/POST public share resource mutation paths after share-root path joining; it does not depend on /public/api/share/info or leaked bearer download URLs.
webdav-access-control-bypass concerns descendant ACL checks being skipped in /dav WebDAV handling and is unrelated to the advisory's share-info disclosure path.
